body.title: Judge agent: inference-based output evaluator (upgrade path from Auditor/Critic)
body.intention: I intend that we have an inference-based judge that evaluates whether an output satisfies an intent, complementing the rules-based Auditor.
body.description:
Hubert (Auditor) and the web Critic are rules-based — they check structural properties against fixed rules. A Judge agent is inference-based: given (intent, normalized constraints, output), it returns a verdict on whether the output satisfies the intent.

This is the LLM-as-judge pattern applied to our sprint outputs. Potential uses:
- Post-sprint: did the shipped code realize the ticket's intention?
- Consequence check: did the predicted outcomes occur?
- Test generation: would a scenario exercise this intent in a failing case?

Observed in working form at a parallel project (test automation; judges generated test cases against intent + constraints).

**Affected files:** TBD — new device or capability in devices/hubert/ or devices/classifier/
**Design rules:** no-autonomous-action (judge advises, does not close tickets or modify code)
**Scope boundary:** IN — verdict (pass/fail/partial) + rationale on (intent, constraints, artifact). OUT — acting on the verdict.
**Completion criteria:** Judge produces pass/fail/partial verdicts with rationale on 5 sprint diffs; Akien spot-checks 3 and agrees with the verdict.
